narrative.recommendation: We recommend authorizing a 25% quota share of Meridian Specialty's habitational property program, subject to the proposed terms below. Pricing supports a sub-65% expected loss ratio with strong collateral protection.
narrative.business_summary: Meridian Specialty Insurance writes admitted habitational (apartment and condo) property across the U.S. Sun Belt through a single appointed MGA. The program is in its fourth year, with $84.2M of subject premium and a stable, granular book of mid-sized risks.
narrative.risk_profile_notes: Exposure is concentrated in the Sun Belt with meaningful named-storm and hail exposure, partially offset by the granular, primary-layer nature of the book.
strengths: Four-year track record with a 58% average gross loss ratio.
Granular book: average TIV per risk of $6.4M, no single risk above $25M.
Disciplined MGA underwriting with binding authority caps and quarterly audits.

weaknesses.point: 32% Florida concentration creates named-storm aggregation risk.
weaknesses.mitigant: Aggregate CAT cap of $40M and 100% reinstatement; modeled 1-in-250 net of cap is within appetite.

weaknesses.point: Reliance on a single MGA for production and underwriting.
weaknesses.mitigant: Binding authority is capped per risk, audited quarterly, and the treaty includes a special termination right on MGA change of control.

noteworthy: Cedent has agreed to a 90/10 trust/operating remittance split.
Loss-sensitive features (sliding scale + profit commission) align interests.

terms.subject_business: Admitted habitational property (apartments and condominiums), U.S. Sun Belt
terms.excess_allowance: 5% on premium ceded above the 110% cap
terms.premium_caps: Subject premium capped at 110% of estimate ($92.6M)
terms.loss_corridor: Cedent retains 100% of losses between 72.5% and 80% LR
terms.eco_xpl_cap: ECO/XPL covered, capped at $5,000,000 in the aggregate
terms.aggregate_loss_ratio_cap: 300% aggregate loss-ratio cap on ceded losses
terms.reporting_requirements: Monthly premium and loss bordereaux within 30 days of month-end; quarterly loss runs and a quarterly large-loss listing (>$1M)
